reasoning: I am forecasting whether the 4-week moving average of initial jobless claims (IC4WSA) will be higher than the 2025-11-09 baseline at 1-week, 1-month, and 3-month horizons. Looking at recent data, there has been a slight downward trend from 240,500 in September 2025 to 237,500 at the end of October 2025. Because a 4-week moving average exhibits positive autocorrelation in its first differences, a recent downward trend implies the next immediate changes are slightly more likely to be negative than positive. Therefore, the 1-week probability of an increase is slightly below 0.5. As the forecast horizon extends to 4 weeks and then 13 weeks, this short-term autocorrelation decays. Furthermore, the broader economic context from early 2025 (unemployment rate falling back to 4.0% after a Sahm Rule scare in 2024) suggests a stable labor market rather than an accelerating deterioration, keeping trends relatively flat. Following the instruction that uncertainty should increase with the horizon, I move my probability back to a perfectly symmetric 50% baseline for the furthest date.
batch_queries: Will the 4-week moving average of initial unemployment claims have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-11-09?
